title: POST /api/login with valid credentials returns token
description: POST to /api/login with JSON body {"email":"admin@mjmetal.co.id","password":"password"}. Expect HTTP 200, response JSON contains "token" field (non-empty string) and "user" object with "email" equal to admin@mjmetal.co.id.

title: POST /api/login with invalid credentials returns 401
description: POST to /api/login with JSON body {"email":"admin@mjmetal.co.id","password":"wrongpassword"}. Expect HTTP 401 or 422 response. The response must NOT contain a "token" field.

title: GET /api/me with valid Bearer token returns authenticated user
description: First POST /api/login with {"email":"admin@mjmetal.co.id","password":"password"} to get Bearer token. Then GET /api/me with Authorization: Bearer <token>. Expect HTTP 200 and JSON user object with field "email" equal to admin@mjmetal.co.id.

title: GET /api/categories without token returns 401
description: GET /api/categories with no Authorization header. Expect HTTP 401 response. This verifies all protected endpoints require authentication.

title: GET /api/categories returns list of categories
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password and use the returned Bearer token. GET /api/categories with Authorization: Bearer <token>. Expect HTTP 200 and a JSON array. The array should contain at least one category object with "id" and "name" fields. Seeded data includes categories like 'Bahan Baku', 'Barang Jadi'.

title: POST /api/categories creates a new category
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. POST /api/categories with Authorization: Bearer <token> and JSON body {"name":"Test Kategori API","description":"Dibuat via API test"}. Expect HTTP 201, response JSON contains "id" (integer) and "name" equal to "Test Kategori API".

title: PUT /api/categories/{id} updates an existing category
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. First POST /api/categories to create category with name "Kategori Update Test". Use the returned id. Then PUT /api/categories/{id} with JSON body {"name":"Kategori Updated","description":"Updated"}. Expect HTTP 200 and response "name" equals "Kategori Updated".

title: DELETE /api/categories/{id} deletes a category
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. First POST /api/categories to create {"name":"Kategori To Delete"}. Use the returned id. Then DELETE /api/categories/{id} with Bearer token. Expect HTTP 200 with message "Deleted". Verify with GET /api/categories that the deleted category is no longer present.

title: POST /api/categories with duplicate name returns 422
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. POST /api/categories with {"name":"Bahan Baku"} (a name that already exists in seed data). Expect HTTP 422 validation error. The response should contain validation error about the name being already taken.

title: POST /api/categories without required name field returns 422
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. POST /api/categories with JSON body {"description":"No name provided"}. Expect HTTP 422 validation error with errors mentioning the "name" field is required.

title: GET /api/units returns list of units
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. GET /api/units with Authorization: Bearer <token>. Expect HTTP 200 and JSON array with unit objects containing "id", "name", "abbreviation". Seeded units include Kilogram (kg), Pieces (pcs), Liter (ltr).

title: POST /api/units creates a new unit
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. POST /api/units with Authorization: Bearer <token> and JSON body {"name":"Unit Test","abbreviation":"ut"}. Expect HTTP 201 and response JSON with "id" and "name" equal to "Unit Test".

title: POST /api/units with duplicate name returns 422
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. POST /api/units with JSON body {"name":"Kilogram","abbreviation":"kg"} (name that already exists in seed data). Expect HTTP 422 validation error about duplicate name.

title: GET /api/suppliers returns list of suppliers
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. GET /api/suppliers with Authorization: Bearer <token>. Expect HTTP 200 and JSON array. Seeded suppliers include PT Indo Baja Steel (SUP-001). Each supplier object should have "id", "code", "name" fields.

title: POST /api/suppliers creates a new supplier
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. POST /api/suppliers with JSON body {"code":"SUP-TEST","name":"Supplier Test API","email":"test@supplier.com","phone":"021-999999","address":"Test Address","contact_person":"Test Person"}. Expect HTTP 201. Response should contain "id" and "code" equal to "SUP-TEST".

title: GET /api/suppliers/{id} returns supplier detail
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. GET /api/suppliers/1 (first seeded supplier). Expect HTTP 200 with JSON object containing "id" equal to 1 and "code" equal to "SUP-001".

title: PUT /api/suppliers/{id} updates supplier details
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. First POST /api/suppliers to create {"code":"SUP-UPD","name":"Supplier Update","email":"upd@test.com","phone":"021-100","address":"Addr","contact_person":"Person"}. Use returned id. PUT /api/suppliers/{id} with body {"code":"SUP-UPD","name":"Supplier Updated Name","email":"updated@test.com","phone":"021-111","address":"New Addr","contact_person":"New Person"}. Expect HTTP 200 and response "name" equals "Supplier Updated Name".

title: POST /api/suppliers with duplicate code returns 422
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. POST /api/suppliers with {"code":"SUP-001","name":"Duplicate Supplier"} (code already in seed data). Expect HTTP 422 validation error about duplicate supplier code.

title: GET /api/warehouses returns list with locations
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. GET /api/warehouses. Expect HTTP 200 and JSON array. Each warehouse should have "id", "code", "name" and a nested "locations" array. Seeded warehouses include WH-BB (Gudang Bahan Baku), WH-PROD, WH-BJ.

title: POST /api/warehouses creates a new warehouse
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. POST /api/warehouses with JSON body {"code":"WH-TEST","name":"Gudang Test","address":"Jl. Test No. 1"}. Expect HTTP 201. Response should contain "id" and "code" equal to "WH-TEST".

title: POST /api/warehouses with duplicate code returns 422
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. POST /api/warehouses with {"code":"WH-BB","name":"Duplicate Warehouse"} (code already in seed data). Expect HTTP 422 validation error about duplicate warehouse code.

title: GET /api/items returns paginated item list
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. GET /api/items. Expect HTTP 200 and JSON pagination object with "data" array. Each item in data should have "id", "code", "name" and nested "category" and "unit" objects. Seeded items include BB-001 (Besi Hollow 40x40x2mm).

title: POST /api/items creates a new inventory item
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. First GET /api/categories to get a valid category_id (e.g., first item's id). Get unit_id from GET /api/units. Then POST /api/items with {"code":"TEST-001","name":"Item Test API","category_id":<valid_id>,"unit_id":<valid_id>,"min_stock":10,"max_stock":100}. Expect HTTP 201. Response has "id", "code" equal to "TEST-001", nested "category" and "unit" objects.

title: GET /api/items/{id} returns item detail with stocks
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. GET /api/items/1 (first seeded item). Expect HTTP 200. Response should have "id" equal to 1, "code" equal to "BB-001" and a nested "stocks" array showing stock quantities per warehouse.

title: PUT /api/items/{id} updates item details
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. First POST /api/items to create item with code "ITEM-UPD", valid category_id and unit_id from the API. Then PUT /api/items/{id} with {"code":"ITEM-UPD","name":"Item Updated Name","category_id":<same>,"unit_id":<same>,"min_stock":20}. Expect HTTP 200 and response "name" equals "Item Updated Name".

title: POST /api/items with invalid category_id returns 422
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. GET /api/units to get a valid unit_id. POST /api/items with {"code":"ITEM-BADCAT","name":"Bad Category Item","category_id":99999,"unit_id":<valid_id>}. Expect HTTP 422 validation error due to non-existent category_id (invalid foreign key).

title: POST /api/items with duplicate code returns 422
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. POST /api/items with {"code":"BB-001","name":"Duplicate Item"} (code already in seed data). Expect HTTP 422 validation error about duplicate item code.

title: POST /api/items missing required fields returns 422
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. POST /api/items with empty body {}. Expect HTTP 422 validation errors. The error response should include validation messages for required fields: "code", "name", "category_id", "unit_id".

title: GET /api/stocks returns current stock levels
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. GET /api/stocks. Expect HTTP 200 and JSON array. Each stock record has "id", "item_id", "warehouse_id", "quantity" fields and nested "item" and "warehouse" objects. Array should be non-empty (seeded data exists).

title: GET /api/stock-movements returns movement history
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. GET /api/stock-movements. Expect HTTP 200 and JSON pagination object with "data" array. Each movement has "id", "type", "quantity", "item_id" fields. Seeded database has 34 stock movements.

title: POST goods_receipt movement increases stock quantity
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. GET /api/stocks?warehouse_id=1&item_id=1 to record the current quantity of item id=1 in warehouse id=1. Then POST /api/stock-movements with {"type":"goods_receipt","item_id":1,"to_warehouse_id":1,"quantity":50,"reference_number":"GR-TEST-001"}. Expect HTTP 201. Response has "type" equal to "goods_receipt". Then GET /api/stocks?warehouse_id=1&item_id=1 and verify new quantity is previous_quantity + 50.

title: POST material_issue movement decreases stock quantity
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. First POST /api/stock-movements with {"type":"goods_receipt","item_id":1,"to_warehouse_id":1,"quantity":100} to ensure stock exists. Then GET /api/stocks?warehouse_id=1&item_id=1 to record current quantity. POST /api/stock-movements with {"type":"material_issue","item_id":1,"from_warehouse_id":1,"quantity":20,"reference_number":"MI-TEST-001"}. Expect HTTP 201. Verify GET /api/stocks?warehouse_id=1&item_id=1 shows quantity decreased by 20.

title: Stock quantity correctness after goods_receipt then material_issue sequence
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. 1) POST goods_receipt: {"type":"goods_receipt","item_id":2,"to_warehouse_id":1,"quantity":200}. 2) POST material_issue: {"type":"material_issue","item_id":2,"from_warehouse_id":1,"quantity":30}. 3) GET /api/stocks?item_id=2&warehouse_id=1. Verify the stock record quantity for item 2 in warehouse 1 reflects net change of +200-30 from these two operations. Also GET /api/reports/stock-summary and verify item with code "BB-002" has total_qty > 0.

title: POST stock_movement with invalid type returns 422
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. POST /api/stock-movements with {"type":"invalid_type","item_id":1,"to_warehouse_id":1,"quantity":10}. Expect HTTP 422 validation error. The error should reference the "type" field for invalid enum value.

title: POST stock_movement without required item_id returns 422
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. POST /api/stock-movements with {"type":"goods_receipt","to_warehouse_id":1,"quantity":10} (missing item_id). Expect HTTP 422 validation with error on "item_id" field.

title: POST /api/purchase-orders creates PO in draft status
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. POST /api/purchase-orders with {"po_number":"PO-TEST-001","supplier_id":1,"warehouse_id":1,"expected_date":"2026-03-30","items":[{"item_id":1,"quantity":50,"unit_price":15000}]}. Expect HTTP 201. Response has "id", "po_number" equal to "PO-TEST-001", "status" equal to "draft", nested "supplier" and "items" arrays.

title: GET /api/purchase-orders lists all purchase orders
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. GET /api/purchase-orders. Expect HTTP 200 and JSON pagination with "data" array. Seeded database has purchase orders. Each PO object has "id", "po_number", "status", "supplier" nested object.

title: GET /api/purchase-orders/{id} returns PO detail
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. GET /api/purchase-orders/1. Expect HTTP 200 with a PO detail object containing "id" equal to 1, "status", nested "supplier", "warehouse", "items" array with "item" sub-objects.

title: PO lifecycle: draft then approve changes status to approved
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. 1) POST /api/purchase-orders with po_number "PO-APPROVE-TEST", supplier_id=1, warehouse_id=1, expected_date="2026-03-30", items=[{"item_id":1,"quantity":10,"unit_price":5000}]. Record the returned id. 2) POST /api/purchase-orders/{id}/approve with Bearer token. Expect HTTP 200. Response "status" equals "approved".

title: PO lifecycle: approve then receive records quantities and increases stock
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. 1) Create PO: POST /api/purchase-orders po_number="PO-RECV-TEST", supplier_id=1, warehouse_id=1, expected_date="2026-03-30", items=[{"item_id":3,"quantity":100,"unit_price":50000}]. 2) POST /api/purchase-orders/{id}/approve. 3) GET /api/purchase-orders/{id} to get purchase_order_item id from items array. 4) GET /api/stocks?item_id=3&warehouse_id=1 to record stock_before. 5) POST /api/purchase-orders/{id}/receive with {"items":[{"purchase_order_item_id":<poi_id>,"received_quantity":100}]}. Expect HTTP 200. 6) GET /api/stocks?item_id=3&warehouse_id=1. Verify quantity >= stock_before + 100.

title: PO lifecycle: cancel changes status to cancelled
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. POST /api/purchase-orders with po_number="PO-CANCEL-TEST", supplier_id=1, warehouse_id=1, expected_date="2026-03-30", items=[{"item_id":1,"quantity":5,"unit_price":1000}]. Record returned id. POST /api/purchase-orders/{id}/cancel. Expect HTTP 200 and response "status" equals "cancelled".

title: POST approve on already-approved PO returns error
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. 1) Create PO po_number="PO-DBLAPP-TEST", supplier_id=1, warehouse_id=1, expected_date="2026-03-30", items=[{"item_id":1,"quantity":5,"unit_price":1000}]. 2) POST approve. 3) POST approve again. Expect HTTP 4xx (400, 422, or 500) error with message indicating PO is not in draft status and cannot be approved again.

title: POST /api/purchase-orders missing required fields returns 422
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. POST /api/purchase-orders with empty body {}. Expect HTTP 422 validation errors mentioning required fields: po_number, supplier_id, warehouse_id, expected_date, items.

title: POST /api/production-orders creates WO in draft status
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. POST /api/production-orders with {"wo_number":"WO-TEST-001","title":"Test Production Order","warehouse_id":1,"planned_start":"2026-03-01","planned_end":"2026-03-10","inputs":[{"item_id":1,"quantity":50},{"item_id":2,"quantity":20}],"outputs":[{"item_id":13,"quantity":10}]}. Expect HTTP 201. Response "status" equals "draft", has nested "inputs" and "outputs" arrays with item details.

title: GET /api/production-orders lists all work orders
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. GET /api/production-orders. Expect HTTP 200 and JSON pagination with "data" array. Seeded database has production orders. Each WO has "id", "wo_number", "status" fields.

title: GET /api/production-orders/{id} returns WO detail with inputs and outputs
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. GET /api/production-orders/1. Expect HTTP 200 with object containing "id" equal to 1, "status", nested "warehouse", "inputs" and "outputs" arrays, each with "item" sub-objects.

title: WO lifecycle: start changes status to in_progress
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. 1) Ensure stock for inputs: POST goods_receipt {"type":"goods_receipt","item_id":1,"to_warehouse_id":1,"quantity":500}. Also POST goods_receipt {"type":"goods_receipt","item_id":2,"to_warehouse_id":1,"quantity":500}. 2) Create WO: POST /api/production-orders wo_number="WO-START-TEST", title="Start Test", warehouse_id=1, planned_start="2026-03-01", planned_end="2026-03-10", inputs=[{"item_id":1,"quantity":5},{"item_id":2,"quantity":5}], outputs=[{"item_id":13,"quantity":1}]. 3) POST /api/production-orders/{id}/start. Expect HTTP 200. Response "status" equals "in_progress".

title: WO lifecycle: start then complete changes status to completed
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. 1) Ensure stock: POST goods_receipt item_id=3, warehouse_id=1, quantity=1000. 2) Create WO: wo_number="WO-COMP-TEST", title="Complete Test", warehouse_id=1, planned_start="2026-03-01", planned_end="2026-03-15", inputs=[{"item_id":3,"quantity":10}], outputs=[{"item_id":14,"quantity":1}]. 3) POST start. 4) POST /api/production-orders/{id}/complete. Expect HTTP 200. Response "status" equals "completed".

title: WO lifecycle: cancel changes status to cancelled
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. Create WO: wo_number="WO-CNCL-TEST", title="Cancel Test", warehouse_id=1, planned_start="2026-03-01", planned_end="2026-03-10". POST /api/production-orders/{id}/cancel. Expect HTTP 200. Response "status" equals "cancelled".

title: POST /api/production-orders with planned_end before planned_start returns 422
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. POST /api/production-orders with {"wo_number":"WO-BADDATE","title":"Bad Date WO","warehouse_id":1,"planned_start":"2026-03-10","planned_end":"2026-03-01"}. Expect HTTP 422 validation error because planned_end is before planned_start.

title: POST start on completed WO returns error
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. 1) Ensure stock: POST goods_receipt item_id=4, warehouse_id=1, quantity=500. 2) Create WO wo_number="WO-DONE-TEST", warehouse_id=1, planned_start="2026-03-01", planned_end="2026-03-15", inputs=[{"item_id":4,"quantity":5}], outputs=[{"item_id":14,"quantity":1}]. 3) POST start. 4) POST complete. 5) POST /api/production-orders/{id}/start again. Expect HTTP 4xx error because WO is already completed and cannot be started again.

title: POST /api/stock-opnames creates opname in draft status
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. POST /api/stock-opnames with {"opname_number":"OP-TEST-001","warehouse_id":1,"counted_at":"2026-02-27","notes":"API test opname"}. Expect HTTP 201. Response "warehouse_id" equals 1 and status is not completed.

title: POST load-stock populates opname with warehouse items
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. 1) Create opname: POST /api/stock-opnames {"opname_number":"OP-LOAD-001","warehouse_id":1,"counted_at":"2026-02-27"}. 2) POST /api/stock-opnames/{id}/load-stock. Expect HTTP 200. Response nested "items" array should be non-empty — populated from stock in warehouse 1. Each item in the array should have "item_id" and "system_quantity".

title: POST save-count stores counted quantities
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. 1) Create opname opname_number="OP-SAVE-001", warehouse_id=1, counted_at="2026-02-27". 2) POST load-stock. 3) From load-stock response items array, get the first item's item_id. 4) POST /api/stock-opnames/{id}/save-count with {"counts":{"<item_id>":99}}. Expect HTTP 200. Response items array should contain counted_quantity equal to 99 for that item_id.

title: POST complete closes opname and applies stock adjustments
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. 1) Create opname opname_number="OP-COMP-001", warehouse_id=1, counted_at="2026-02-27". 2) POST load-stock. 3) Get item_id from first loaded item. 4) POST save-count {"counts":{"<item_id>":150}}. 5) POST /api/stock-opnames/{id}/complete. Expect HTTP 200. Response "status" equals "completed". GET /api/stock-movements?type=stock_adjustment to verify an adjustment movement was created.

title: POST cancel changes opname status to cancelled
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. Create opname opname_number="OP-CNCL-001", warehouse_id=1, counted_at="2026-02-27". POST /api/stock-opnames/{id}/cancel. Expect HTTP 200 and response "status" equals "cancelled".

title: GET /api/reports/stock-summary returns all items with total quantities
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. GET /api/reports/stock-summary. Expect HTTP 200 and JSON array. Each item has "id", "code", "name", "total_qty" (number or null), nested "category" and "unit". The array count should be >= 16 (seeded items). At least one item should have total_qty > 0.

title: GET /api/reports/low-stock returns items below minimum stock
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. GET /api/reports/low-stock. Expect HTTP 200 and JSON array. Each returned item should have "min_stock" set and "total_qty" less than "min_stock" (COALESCE(total_qty, 0) < min_stock). Verify structure with "id", "code", "min_stock", "total_qty".

title: GET /api/reports/movement-history with type filter returns filtered results
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. GET /api/reports/movement-history?type=goods_receipt. Expect HTTP 200 and JSON pagination with "data" array. All items in data should have "type" equal to "goods_receipt". Verify at least one result exists in seeded data.

title: GET /api/users with admin token returns user list
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. GET /api/users with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token>. Expect HTTP 200 and JSON array of users. Each user has "id", "name", "email", "role". The array should have at least 5 users (seeded). Verify admin@mjmetal.co.id appears in the list.

title: POST /api/users creates a new user (admin only)
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. POST /api/users with {"name":"Test User API","email":"testuser@mjmetal.co.id","password":"password123","role":"warehouse_operator"}. Expect HTTP 201. Response has "id", "name" equal to "Test User API", "role" equal to "warehouse_operator". Password field should not appear in response.

title: PUT /api/users/{id} updates user details (admin only)
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. First POST /api/users to create user with email "updatetest@mjmetal.co.id", name="Update Test", password="password123", role="warehouse_operator". Use returned id. PUT /api/users/{id} with {"name":"Updated Name","email":"updatetest@mjmetal.co.id","role":"supervisor"}. Expect HTTP 200 and response "name" equals "Updated Name", "role" equals "supervisor".

title: DELETE /api/users/{id} deletes a user (admin only)
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. First POST /api/users to create user email="todelete@mjmetal.co.id", name="To Delete", password="password123", role="warehouse_operator". Use returned id. DELETE /api/users/{id}. Expect HTTP 200 with response message "Deleted".

title: DELETE own admin account returns 422 cannot delete self
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. GET /api/me to get the current admin user id. DELETE /api/users/{own_id} with Bearer token. Expect HTTP 422. Response message should indicate cannot delete yourself.

title: GET /api/users with non-admin token returns 403 Forbidden
description: Login as inventory@mjmetal.co.id / password (role: inventory_controller, not admin). Use the returned Bearer token. GET /api/users with Authorization: Bearer <inventory_token>. Expect HTTP 403 Forbidden. Non-admin roles are blocked from user management endpoints.

title: POST /api/users with non-admin token returns 403 Forbidden
description: Login as gudang@mjmetal.co.id / password (role: warehouse_operator, not admin). Use the returned Bearer token. POST /api/users with body {"name":"Unauthorized","email":"unauth@test.com","password":"password123","role":"warehouse_operator"}. Expect HTTP 403 Forbidden response.

title: POST /api/users with duplicate email returns 422
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. POST /api/users with {"name":"Dup Email","email":"admin@mjmetal.co.id","password":"password123","role":"warehouse_operator"} (email already exists in seed data). Expect HTTP 422 validation error about duplicate email address.

title: POST /api/users with invalid role value returns 422
description: Login as admin@mjmetal.co.id / password. POST /api/users with {"name":"Bad Role","email":"badrole@mjmetal.co.id","password":"password123","role":"superadmin"} (role not in allowed values: admin, inventory_controller, warehouse_operator, supervisor, production_staff). Expect HTTP 422 validation error about invalid role value.
